name: Cambridge Credit Counseling Corporation
description_short: Non-profit credit counseling and debt management organization offering free credit advice, debt consolidation plans, and HUD-approved housing counseling services.
description_long:
Cambridge Credit Counseling is a non-profit organization that provides comprehensive financial counseling and debt management services to consumers. The organization operates as a full-service financial counseling agency rather than a for-profit debt settlement or lending company, focusing on education and structured repayment solutions.

The company offers multiple specialized services including free credit counseling, debt management plans (DMP), HUD-approved housing counseling, student loan counseling, reverse mortgage counseling, and bankruptcy education courses. Their primary offering is debt management plans where clients consolidate unsecured debts into a single monthly payment. According to their website, they work with major creditors to negotiate reduced interest rates (claiming average reductions from 29% to 8%) and lower monthly payments (averaging 35% or more savings), with typical repayment timelines of 42 months or less.

Cambridge differentiates itself by being HUD-certified for housing counseling and EOUST-approved for bankruptcy counseling, indicating third-party regulatory validation. They emphasize their team of "nationally certified debt experts" and provide free initial debt evaluations. The organization positions itself as offering "the safest way to eliminate unsecured debts" through legitimate creditor negotiation rather than settlement or litigation-based approaches.

As a non-profit entity, Cambridge Credit Counseling is appropriate for consumers seeking structured debt repayment with professional guidance and creditor negotiation support. However, potential clients should understand that debt management plans require creditor acceptance and work best for those with stable income who can commit to multi-year repayment schedules. The company's claims about interest rate reductions and monthly savings are presented as typical outcomes but individual results will vary based on creditor cooperation and personal financial circumstances.
pros: Non-profit status with HUD and EOUST government certifications validates organizational legitimacy
Free initial credit counseling and debt evaluation—no upfront costs for assessment
Offers comprehensive financial services beyond debt management (housing, student loans, bankruptcy, reverse mortgages)
Claims average interest rate reductions from 29% to 8% and 35%+ monthly payment savings through creditor negotiation
Nationally certified debt experts provide personalized financial analysis rather than automated services
Debt management plans consolidate multiple creditor payments into single monthly payment
Typical debt elimination in 42 months or less with structured repayment timeline
cons: Debt management plans require creditor acceptance—not guaranteed and some creditors may refuse enrollment
Debt consolidation through DMP differs from debt settlement; clients pay full balance, not reduced settlements
Website lacks specific information about fees, company history, number of clients served, or success rate statistics
Claims of 29% to 8% interest rate reductions presented as 'typical' but no documentation of how often this is achieved
Long commitment required (average 42 months)—not suitable for consumers needing faster debt resolution
best_for: Consumers with multiple credit card debts seeking structured, creditor-negotiated repayment plans
Homeowners interested in HUD-approved housing counseling, first-time homebuyer courses, or foreclosure prevention
Individuals with federal student loans needing comprehensive debt analysis and repayment strategy guidance
Senior citizens exploring reverse mortgage options who need certified HECM counseling before proceeding
diagnosis: Cambridge Credit Counseling is best suited for debt-burdened consumers with stable income who want professional creditor negotiation without bankruptcy or settlement approaches, and who can commit to multi-year repayment plans. The main caveat is that debt management plan success depends on individual creditor acceptance of enrollment, and actual interest rate reductions and timeline outcomes will vary significantly from advertised typical results.
services: Free credit counseling with in-depth financial situation analysis
Debt management plans with creditor negotiation and single monthly payment consolidation
HUD-approved housing counseling and foreclosure prevention
Mortgage Ready program for homeownership preparation
Student loan counseling and federal loan strategy review
Post-filing debtor education course for bankruptcy completion
Free debt evaluation and savings assessment
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
